subject: Ecology
title: Upwelling: Nutrient Highways
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Upwelling: Nutrient Highways.

hook.text: Some of the richest fishing grounds on Earth sit where cold, deep water rises to the surface.

buildup.text: Upwelling brings nutrients from the ocean floor into the sunlit zone where plankton bloom.

discovery.text: Peru's coast is one example—upwelling supports one of the world's largest fisheries.

twist.text: El Niño shuts down upwelling by warming surface waters, collapsing fish stocks in months.

climax.text: Upwelling zones cover just 1% of the ocean but produce roughly half the world's fish catch.

punchline.text: The richest seas are fed from below.

quiz.question: What does upwelling bring to the ocean surface?
